topic: Why the future belongs to individuals who build systems, not employees who trade time
core_idea: The internet has made it possible for one person to generate the income of a small company. The key is building systems that work while you sleep.
key_insight: Stop selling time. Start selling outcomes.

topic: How to develop monk-like focus in a world designed to distract you
core_idea: Focus is the new IQ. The ability to direct your attention intentionally is the most valuable skill in the modern economy.
key_insight: Your attention is your most valuable asset. Guard it like your life depends on it.

topic: Bringing it all together: identity, systems, and purpose
core_idea: After 177 days of exploration, the synthesis is clear: who you are determines what you build, and what you build determines who you become.
key_insight: Identity and output are in a feedback loop. Change one, and you change both.

topic: Why most people never examine their assumptions — and pay the ultimate price
core_idea: Socrates said the unexamined life is not worth living. In the modern context, this means questioning every default: your career, your relationships, your beliefs about money, success, and meaning. Most people inherit their worldview from their environment and never question it.
key_insight: The quality of your life is determined by the quality of your questions. Stop accepting inherited answers.

topic: What comes after mastery: the infinite game of self-actualization
core_idea: Mastery is not a destination but a direction. The final frontier is not achievement but becoming.
key_insight: The goal is not to arrive. The goal is to keep moving toward your highest self.
